task_completion.description: Create the four specified bookmark folders and sort existing bookmarks into them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Chrome Bookmark Manager (chrome://bookmarks) or the bookmarks bar showing the folders 'Live Radar', 'Satellite Imaging', 'Forecast Models', and 'Off-Air', ideally with bookmarks visible inside them.

task_completion.description: Allow Location access for radar.weather.gov and set global Notifications and Pop-ups to Block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of Chrome Site Settings showing 'https://radar.weather.gov' in the Allowed list for Location, and the default behavior for Notifications and Pop-ups/redirects set to 'Don't allow' (Block).

task_completion.description: Set the download directory to ~/Documents/Weather_Graphics and enable instant saving.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of Chrome Downloads settings (chrome://settings/downloads) showing the Location path ending in 'Weather_Graphics' and the toggle for 'Ask where to save each file before downloading' turned OFF.

task_completion.description: Create a custom site search for 'metar'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of Chrome Manage search engines and site search (chrome://settings/searchEngines) showing a new entry with the keyword 'metar' and the URL 'https://aviationweather.gov/data/metar/?id=%s'.

task_completion.description: Configure the browser to open specific weather URLs on startup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of Chrome On startup settings (chrome://settings/onStartup) with 'Open a specific page or set of pages' selected, listing 'radar.weather.gov' and 'star.nesdis.noaa.gov'.

task_completion.description: Disable password saving and autofill features.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of Chrome Password Manager and Autofill settings showing toggles like 'Offer to save passwords', 'Auto Sign-in', and 'Save and fill addresses/payment methods' turned OFF.

integrity.description: The agent used the Chrome GUI to configure settings rather than directly editing Chrome preference JSON files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Chrome browser settings pages being navigated and modified. There should be no terminal windows open editing the Chrome 'Preferences' file using text editors (nano/vim) or JSON processors (jq).

integrity.description: The agent actually entered the configurations into the Chrome settings fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Text fields in Chrome settings (like Site Search URL, Startup pages) contain the correct values. The agent did not just open the 'weather_wall_config.txt' file to display the target values without applying them.

integrity.description: The agent did not run a pre-written script to automate the browser configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of python or bash scripts being executed in the terminal to interact with Chrome via Selenium, Puppeteer, or direct file modification.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected bookmark folders: 'Live Radar', 'Satellite Imaging', 'Forecast Models', and 'Off-Air'. Target download directory: 'Weather_Graphics'. Custom search engine keyword: 'metar' with URL 'https://aviationweather.gov/data/metar/?id=%s'. Target startup URLs must include 'radar.weather.gov' and 'star.nesdis.noaa.gov'.
